prompt: I have a dataset with about 5,000 images. I want to see what's actually in it — what kinds of images, how the diagnoses are distributed, whether there are any unlabeled ones. The dataset RID is 3-XYZ9.

assertions.name: reads_dataset_overview
assertions.description: Should read dataset resource for metadata overview
assertions.check: reads deriva://dataset/3-XYZ9 or calls get_record for the dataset

assertions.name: lists_members_by_element_type
assertions.description: Should list members to discover which element types (tables) have data

assertions.name: uses_denormalize_for_actual_data
assertions.description: Should use denormalize_dataset to see actual data values, not just RIDs
assertions.check: calls denormalize_dataset with dataset_rid 3-XYZ9 and include_tables

assertions.name: includes_feature_table_in_denormalize
assertions.description: Should include a diagnosis/feature table in the denormalization to see label distributions
assertions.check: include_tables contains a feature or diagnosis-related table alongside Image

assertions.name: checks_features_or_annotations
assertions.description: Should check what features/annotations exist on Image records
assertions.check: calls fetch_table_features for Image or reads feature resources

assertions.name: addresses_unlabeled_detection
assertions.description: Should explain how to find images that lack labels (unlabeled ones)
assertions.check: mentions finding missing/null values, counting unlabeled records, or comparing labeled vs total counts

assertions.name: follows_browse_pattern
assertions.description: Should follow overview→members→denormalize flow, not just dump RIDs
assertions.check: steps progress from metadata to member listing to actual data viewing in a logical sequence
